pull_request.title: feat(pygal): implement lollipop-basic
pull_request.body:
## Implementation: `lollipop-basic` - pygal

Implements the **pygal** version of `lollipop-basic`.

**File:** `plots/lollipop-basic/implementations/pygal.py`

---
:robot: *[impl-generate workflow](https://github.com/MarkusNeusinger/pyplots/actions/runs/20464354570)*

changed_files.patch:
@@ -1,65 +1,64 @@
 """ pyplots.ai
 lollipop-basic: Basic Lollipop Chart
 Library: pygal 3.1.0 | Python 3.13.11
-Quality: 90/100 | Created: 2025-12-15
+Quality: 91/100 | Created: 2025-12-23
 """
 
 import pygal
 from pygal.style import Style
 
 
-# Data - Product sales by category (sorted by value)
+# Data - Product sales by category (sorted by value for better readability)
 categories = ["Smartphones", "Laptops", "Tablets", "Headphones", "Smartwatches", "Cameras", "Speakers", "Gaming"]
 values = [892, 654, 478, 312, 287, 198, 156, 134]
 
-# Sort by value descending for better readability
+# Sort by value descending (already sorted in this example)
 sorted_data = sorted(zip(categories, values, strict=True), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
 categories = [item[0] for item in sorted_data]
 values = [item[1] for item in sorted_data]
 
-# Custom style for 4800x2700 canvas
-# Use tuple with same color repeated for all 8 series to ensure consistent color
+# Custom style for 4800x2700 canvas with visible stroke
 custom_style = Style(
     background="white",
     plot_background="white",
     foreground="#333333",
     foreground_strong="#333333",
     foreground_subtle="#666666",
-    colors=("#306998",) * 8,  # Python Blue for all series
+    colors=("#306998",) * len(categories),  # Python Blue for all series
     title_font_size=72,
     label_font_size=48,
     major_label_font_size=42,
     legend_font_size=42,
     value_font_size=36,
     value_label_font_size=36,
+    stroke_width=8,  # Line width in style
 )
 
-# Create XY chart for lollipop visualization
-# Each lollipop is a line from (0, y) to (value, y) with a dot at the end
+# Create horizontal lollipop chart using XY
+# Categories on y-axis, values on x-axis (horizontal orientation)
 n = len(categories)
 chart = pygal.XY(
     width=4800,
     height=2700,
     title="lollipop-basic · pygal · pyplots.ai",
     x_title="Sales (units)",
     style=custom_style,
-    show_legend=False,  # Category names shown on y-axis
-    dots_size=30,  # Large dots for clearly visible lollipop heads
-    stroke_style={"width": 8},  # Visible stems at 4800px width
+    show_legend=False,
+    dots_size=24,
+    stroke=True,  # Enable stroke/lines
     show_y_guides=True,
     show_x_guides=True,
     margin=100,
-    xrange=(0, max(values) * 1.1),  # X-axis range
-    range=(0, n + 1),  # Y-axis range to fit all categories
+    xrange=(0, max(values) * 1.1),
+    range=(0.5, n + 0.5),
     y_labels=[{"label": cat, "value": n - i} for i, cat in enumerate(categories)],
 )
 
-# Create lollipop data - each category is a line from 0 to value
-# Using node config to hide dot at baseline, only show dot at value end
+# Add lollipop data - each is a line from x=0 to value
 for i, (cat, val) in enumerate(zip(categories, values, strict=True)):
-    y_pos = n - i  # Position from top to bottom (highest value at top)
-    # Line from baseline (x=0) to value with dot only at value end
-    chart.add(cat, [{"value": (0, y_pos), "node": {"r": 0}}, {"value": (val, y_pos)}], dots_size=30)
+    y_pos = n - i
+    # Hide dot at baseline (r=0), show large dot at value end
+    chart.add(cat, [{"value": (0, y_pos), "node": {"r": 0}}, {"value": (val, y_pos)}])
 
 # Save outputs
 chart.render_to_png("plot.png")

@@ -1,17 +1,24 @@
 library: pygal
 specification_id: lollipop-basic
-created: 2025-12-15 16:07:20+00:00
-updated: 2025-12-15 16:07:20+00:00
+created: '2025-12-23T15:14:09Z'
+updated: '2025-12-23T15:20:53Z'
 generated_by: claude-opus-4-5-20251101
-workflow_run: 20238744271
-issue: 934
+workflow_run: 20464354570
+issue: 0
 python_version: 3.13.11
 library_version: 3.1.0
 preview_url: https://storage.googleapis.com/pyplots-images/plots/lollipop-basic/pygal/plot.png
 preview_thumb: https://storage.googleapis.com/pyplots-images/plots/lollipop-basic/pygal/plot_thumb.png
 preview_html: https://storage.googleapis.com/pyplots-images/plots/lollipop-basic/pygal/plot.html
-quality_score: 90
+quality_score: 91
 review:
-  strengths: []
-  weaknesses: []
-  improvements: []
+  strengths:
+  - Excellent implementation of lollipop chart using pygal XY chart with creative
+    baseline dot hiding technique
+  - Clean horizontal orientation that handles category labels well
+  - Proper title format and color scheme following pyplots standards
+  - Data sorted by value for optimal readability as recommended in spec
+  - Good canvas utilization at 4800x2700 with appropriate font scaling
+  weaknesses:
+  - Missing y_title axis label (could add Product Category or similar)
+  - Font sizes in Style could be better optimized for the large canvas

commit_summaries.message: chore(pygal): update quality score 91 and review feedback for lollipop-basic
